pattern: Counting days elapsed from a known anchor date and reducing modulo seven names the weekday without any calendar library.
hints: Pick an anchor date whose weekday you know, then the weekday of any later date is just the day-count gap reduced modulo seven.
To count days between two dates, accumulate full years (adding a leap day for each leap year) plus the months already passed this year plus the day of month.
A year is a leap year when divisible by 4 but not 100, unless also divisible by 400; sum the days since the anchor and index into the weekday names.

edge_inputs.description: the anchor day itself, a Friday
leap-day Feb 29 in a divisible-by-400 year
March 1 right after a leap day
year divisible by 100 but not 400 is not leap

stress.description: random valid dates across the whole supported range
